Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotUnknownElement

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotElement
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotUnknownElement
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPotElement
public class PotUnknownElement extends PotElement
Represents a PowerPoint slide element whose specific type is not recognized by the PotShaper library.

This class encapsulates an Apache POI XSLFShape that does not correspond to any of the known PotElement subclasses (such as PotTextBox or PotPicture). It provides basic geometric manipulation and type introspection, but advanced operations like duplication are unsupported. Use this class to handle shapes from newer PowerPoint versions or custom extensions that are not yet directly modeled.

Usage Example


 for (PotElement element : slide.getElements()) {
     if (element instanceof PotUnknownElement) {
         PotUnknownElement unknown = (PotUnknownElement) element;
         System.out.println("Unknown element type: " + unknown.getTypeName());
     }
 }

Key capabilities include:

  • Positioning and resizing via fluent setters inherited from PotElement.
  • Identifying the underlying POI shape class name.
  • Conditional text access if the shape is actually a text shape.
  • Direct access to the raw POI XSLFShape via getRawShape().
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

    • rotate

      public PotUnknownElement rotate(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotation

      public PotUnknownElement rotation(double angle)

      Sets the rotation angle of the element (fluent builder alias).
      Overrides:
      rotation in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • getTypeName

      public String getTypeName()
      Returns the simple class name of the underlying POI shape.
      Returns:
      the simple class name (e.g., "XSLFGraphicFrame")

    • getFullTypeName

      public String getFullTypeName()
      Returns the fully qualified class name of the underlying POI shape.
      Returns:
      the fully qualified class name (e.g., "org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XSLFGraphicFrame")

    • isType

      public boolean isType(String className)
      Checks whether the underlying shape's class name matches the given string.

      The comparison is performed against both the simple name and the fully qualified name.

      Parameters:
      className - the class name to compare against (may be simple or fully qualified)
      Returns:
      true if the shape's simple or fully qualified class name equals className, false otherwise or if className is null

    • isInstanceOf

      public boolean isInstanceOf(Class<?> clazz)
      Determines if the underlying POI shape is an instance of the specified class.
      Parameters:
      clazz - the class to test against
      Returns:
      true if the underlying shape is an instance of clazz, false otherwise or if clazz is null

    • tryGetText

      public String tryGetText()
      Attempts to retrieve text content if the underlying shape is a text shape.

      This method safely checks whether the wrapped XSLFShape is actually an XSLFTextShape. If so, it returns the shape's text; otherwise it returns null.

      Returns:
      the text content if the shape is a text shape, null otherwise
      See Also:
      • XSLFTextShape.getText()

    • trySetText

      public boolean trySetText(String text)
      Attempts to set text content if the underlying shape is a text shape.

      This method safely checks whether the wrapped XSLFShape is actually an XSLFTextShape. If so, it sets the shape's text and returns true; otherwise it returns false and does nothing.

      Parameters:
      text - the new text to set
      Returns:
      true if the text was successfully set, false if the shape is not a text shape
      See Also:
      • XSLFTextShape.setText(String)

    • duplicate

      public PotElement duplicate()
      Duplication is not supported for unknown element types.
      Specified by:
      duplicate in interface IPotElement
      Overrides:
      duplicate in class PotElement
      Returns:
      a new element that is a duplicate of this one
      Throws:
      PotException - always, with an "unsupported operation" message
      See Also:
      • PotElement.duplicate()

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class PotElement
